description_long: This object is a tall, cylindrical bollard with a uniform diameter from top to bottom, constructed from what appears to be concrete or a similar composite material. The surface is predominantly gray with irregular, darker patches and streaks, suggesting weathering or exposure to urban grime. The top of the bollard is flat and unadorned, with no visible cap or decorative elements. The base is also flat, indicating it is likely designed to be embedded or surface-mounted for stability. There are no visible chains, connectors, or reflective markings on the exterior. The overall appearance is utilitarian, suitable for controlling vehicle access or protecting pedestrian areas in urban environments.
description: A tall, gray cylindrical bollard with a flat top and base, featuring weathered, dark streaks on its hard concrete surface.
description_view_0: The cylindrical bollard is shown with visible dark streaks and smudges along its surface, especially prominent on the lower half.
description_view_1: This view highlights a large, irregular dark patch on the side, with the rest of the surface appearing smoother and less marked.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this angle shows the same dark streaks and smudges, with the cylindrical shape and flat top clearly visible.
description_view_3: This view again emphasizes the large dark patch seen in view 1, with minimal additional markings elsewhere.
